REPLY IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 62947476) FILED BY DEFENDANT SUTTER BAY HOSPITALS DOING BUSINESS AS CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SUTTER BAY HOSPITALS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. LIBERALLY CONSTRUING THE EVIDENCE PRESENTED BY PLAINTIFF BARBARA TRYON, THAT EVIDENCE SHOWN THAT THERE ARE TRIABLE DISPUTES WHETHER: 1) THE COMBINATION OF THE CARPET, GAP, AND LIP OF THE TRIM CONSTITUTES A DANGEROUS CONDITION OF PROPERTY (WILLIAMSON DEPO 63:14-23; 68:2-19; 69:22-71:13) AND 2) THAT CONDITION CAUSED MS. TRYON'S INJURY (WILLIAMSON DEPO 69:22:1-71:13). WHILE SUTTER'S REPLY MEMORANDUM ARGUES THAT MS. TRYON HAS NO EVIDENCE ON THE ELEMENT OF NOTICE, SUTTER'S NOTICE OF MOTION DOES NOT MENTION THAT ISSUE NOR WAS THAT ISSUE ARGUED IN SUTTER'S INITIAL MEMORANDUM AND THUS IT CANNOT BE A BASIS FOR GRANTING THIS MOTION. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE RULING. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED
DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF BARBARA TRYON'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING PRODUCTION OF DPE REPORT IS OFF CALENDAR AS A DUPLICATE ENTRY. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Continue Trial Date
Off Calendar Mar-14-2019 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-14-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James J. O'Donnell, Esq., Telephone 925-935-1707 appearing for Plaintiff (CourtCall)
No other appearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SUTTER BAY HOSPITALS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Defendant Sutter Bay Hospitals' motion for summary judgment is denied. Liberally construing the evidence presented by plaintiff Barbara Tryon, that evidence shown that there are triable disputes whether: 1) the combination of the carpet, gap, and lip of the trim constitutes a dangerous condition of property (Williamson Depo 63:14-23; 68:2-19; 69:22-71:13) and 2) that condition caused Ms. Tryon's injury (Williamson Depo 69:22:1-71:13). While Sutter's reply memorandum argues that Ms. Tryon has no evidence on the element of notice, Sutter's notice of motion does not mention that issue nor was that issue argued in Sutter's initial memorandum and thus it cannot be a basis for granting this motion.

Counsel for plaintiffis required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312 and CCP 473c(g)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not reported (302/HEK)

Notice Of Motion For Protective Order And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-01-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

Ruling - The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Defendants' motion is denied. Defendants have failed to show "good cause" for excluding Mr. Williamson from Plaintiff's deposition - the mere fact that Mr. Williamson is a percipient witness is not adequate good cause to exclude him. CCP 2025.420. The Court is concerned, however, about the role of Mr. Williamson at the deposition. Plaintiff's counsel suggests that Mr. Williamson will "assist [counsel] in understanding what [Plaintiff] is saying or when she is confused." If as the deposition proceeds it appears that the presence of Mr. Williamson is impeding efforts to obtain Plaintiff's own honest testimony based on her personal recollection, or otherwise interfering with or hindering the examination, then Defendants may renew their motion to exclude Mr. Williamson from the deposition. The Court finds that there is substantial justification for Defendants' positions and therefore the Plaintiff's request for discovery sanctions is denied. Plaintiff is to prepare a form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and submit it to Judge Pro Tem Roger Mead for review and signature.

Judge Pro Tem: Roger Mead, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. (302/JPT/HK)
